query_text:
Retrieve the most relevant algebra instructional evidence for this pedagogical RAG tutoring turn.

Task ID: CP-02
Turn number: 2
Algebra category: Short conceptual prompt
Topic: short conceptual prompt
Subskill: purpose of simplification
Original student-facing task: Why is it important to combine like terms before solving some equations?
Current learner message: Student says: "Is it just to make the answer shorter?"
Selected pedagogical response mode: procedural_hint

Prioritize evidence that supports:
- mathematically accurate explanation,
- misconception correction where relevant,
- stepwise tutoring support,
- the selected pedagogical response mode.

retrieved_chunks.title: Guiding question for combining like terms
retrieved_chunks.content: Before combining terms, ask: Do these terms have the same variable part? If yes, combine the coefficients. If no, keep them separate.
retrieved_chunks.retrieval_excerpt:
Title: Guiding question for combining like terms
chunk_id: ALG-CP-001
corpus_version: v1.0
topic: short conceptual prompt
subskill: like terms reasoning
difficulty: easy
resource_type: guided_question
misconception_tag: combining_unlike_terms
supports_tasks: CP-01, SE-05
pedagogical_use: conceptual_prompt, light_hint

Instructional content:
Before combining terms, ask: Do these terms have the same variable part? If yes, combine the coefficients. If no, keep them separate.

retrieved_chunks.title: Combining like terms with variables
retrieved_chunks.content: Like terms have the same variable part raised to the same power. For example, 3x and 5x are like terms because both contain x. To combine them, add the coefficients: 3x + 5x = 8x. A constant term such as -2 does not combine with x-terms because it has no variable.
retrieved_chunks.retrieval_excerpt:
Title: Combining like terms with variables
chunk_id: ALG-SE-001
corpus_version: v1.0
topic: simplifying expressions
subskill: combining like terms
difficulty: easy
resource_type: conceptual_explanation
misconception_tag: incorrect_coefficient_addition
supports_tasks: SE-01
pedagogical_use: conceptual_prompt, light_hint, misconception_correction

Instructional content:
Like terms have the same variable part raised to the same power. For example, 3x and 5x are like terms because both contain x. To combine them, add the coefficients: 3x + 5x = 8x. A constant term such as -2 does not combine with x-terms because it has no variable.

retrieved_chunks.title: Why 3x plus 5x is not 6x
retrieved_chunks.content: A common error is adding 3x and 5x incorrectly. The coefficients 3 and 5 tell how many x-groups there are. Three x-groups plus five x-groups gives eight x-groups, so 3x + 5x = 8x, not 6x.
retrieved_chunks.retrieval_excerpt:
Title: Why 3x plus 5x is not 6x
chunk_id: ALG-SE-002
corpus_version: v1.0
topic: simplifying expressions
subskill: combining like terms
difficulty: easy
resource_type: misconception_note
misconception_tag: incorrect_coefficient_addition
supports_tasks: SE-01
pedagogical_use: misconception_correction

Instructional content:
A common error is adding 3x and 5x incorrectly. The coefficients 3 and 5 tell how many x-groups there are. Three x-groups plus five x-groups gives eight x-groups, so 3x + 5x = 8x, not 6x.

retrieved_chunks.title: Like and unlike terms
retrieved_chunks.content: Terms can be combined only when they are like terms. Variable terms such as 5m and 2m can combine because both have m. Constants such as -3 and 9 can combine because neither has a variable. But 5m and 9 cannot combine because one has a variable and the other does not.
retrieved_chunks.retrieval_excerpt:
Title: Like and unlike terms
chunk_id: ALG-SE-006
corpus_version: v1.0
topic: simplifying expressions
subskill: like and unlike terms
difficulty: easy
resource_type: conceptual_explanation
misconception_tag: combining_unlike_terms
supports_tasks: SE-05
pedagogical_use: conceptual_prompt, light_hint, misconception_correction

Instructional content:
Terms can be combined only when they are like terms. Variable terms such as 5m and 2m can combine because both have m. Constants such as -3 and 9 can combine because neither has a variable. But 5m and 9 cannot combine because one has a variable and the other does not.

retrieved_chunks.title: Distributive property before combining like terms
retrieved_chunks.content: When simplifying an expression with parentheses, distribute first, then combine like terms. Example: 4(a + 3) - 2a becomes 4a + 12 - 2a. Then combine the like terms 4a and -2a to get 2a + 12.
retrieved_chunks.retrieval_excerpt:
Title: Distributive property before combining like terms
chunk_id: ALG-SE-003
corpus_version: v1.0
topic: simplifying expressions
subskill: distributive property and combining like terms
difficulty: medium
resource_type: worked_example
misconception_tag: distribution_or_combining_error
supports_tasks: SE-02, SE-03
pedagogical_use: procedural_hint, worked_step_explanation

Instructional content:
When simplifying an expression with parentheses, distribute first, then combine like terms. Example: 4(a + 3) - 2a becomes 4a + 12 - 2a. Then combine the like terms 4a and -2a to get 2a + 12.

metadata.subskill: distributive property and combining like terms

Use the File Search store to retrieve the most relevant algebra instructional evidence for the request below.
